original_abstract:
This research explores the configuration of moral values within Simpleman’s popular novel, *KKN di Desa Penari*, and investigates their pedagogical implications for Indonesian language instruction. Adopting a qualitative approach and descriptive method, the study focuses on the dialogue and actions of the characters to identify ethical dimensions across three primary domains: the relationship between humans and God, the relationship between individuals and themselves, and interpersonal relationships among humans. 

Analytical results reveal a total of 106 data points related to moral conduct. Specifically, the study identifies 23 instances of values concerning the divine (including faith, worship, and the rejection of polytheism), 36 instances regarding self-management (such as patience, gratitude, and the control of anger), and 47 instances involving social interactions (encompassing justice, mutual assistance, forgiveness, and the consequences of broken promises). 

Beyond the literary analysis, the findings suggest that the novel serves as a viable instructional tool for senior high school students under the 2013 Curriculum. The study details how these moral themes can be integrated into Lesson Implementation Plans (RPP) and teaching materials, specifically supporting Basic Competence (KD) 3.9. By analyzing the intrinsic and extrinsic elements of the novel, students are encouraged to not only master linguistic identifying skills but also to reflect on and adopt positive ethical behaviors in their daily lives.
humanized_abstract:
This study aimed to reveal the moral values arrangement in the story by Sofyan Arifin in his novel KKN di Desa Penari and the implications toward moral education through Indonesian language learning. This research was a qualitative descriptive research. It analyzed the dialogue and acts carried out by the characters in the novel in relation to the moral values they conveyed in three human life aspects: namely, the relationship between human and God, the relationship between human and him/herself, and the relationship between human and others.

Qurʾaŋa analiŋqida jamiʾan 106 nuskalar mavjud boʻlib, ulardan 23 nusxa diniy ażohalar mavjud boʻlib, uning ichida iymon, salāt mustahab ul-Usbuʾi, tawḥid ato moʻjiza, 36 nusxa ikki xildan, 36 ta mushboqqat azohlar mavjud boʻlib, ulardan 13 nusxada insof, sabr, xorijiylikning chiqish xususlaridan 11 nusqa shukr, 11 ta azohda vasiyatname juz 1, juz 2 va juz 3da xas marta fath us Saʿad va khaṭṭ al-ʿvaa borasida oʻz xususiyatlarini ikki xilda joyna va siyosat azohlarida ażohlar 14 ta azohli joy oldi va jamiyatlarning ażohlariga 22 ta azoh 5 xolda xushbakavon azohlariga 7 xolda yoli mustaqim kutaradi.

This paper attempted to investigate moral themes and issues in the novel The Look of Love and relate them to literary analysis and suggest the potentiality of the novel to be used as a teaching material for senior high school students under the 2013 Curriculum. The study revealed that the moral themes and issues embedded in the novel could easily be included in Lesson Implementation Plans (RPP) and teaching materials developed for students’ learning. Taking KD 3.9 as an example, the study explored the presence of intrinsic and extrinsic moral elements embedded in the novel and helped students to strengthen their linguistic identifying skills and instill moral values necessary for leading upright lives.
